# Substrate Evolution Log V1

Status: replay-safe governance evidence.

Purpose: track the effectiveness of the executable governance primitive substrate without changing runtime behavior or adding new primitives.

Source lineage:

- `docs/governance/EXECUTABLE_GOVERNANCE_PRIMITIVE_EVOLUTION_V1.md`

This log is evidence-only. It does not modify runtime behavior, create new primitives, introduce orchestration, or expand operational authority.

## Log Entry 001 - Primitive Substrate Stabilization

Scope:

- governed capability memory;
- governed preview runtime preparation;
- governed test execution preparation;
- replay lineage strengthening.

Result:

- substrate remains bounded;
- primitives remain non-executing;
- repeated operational instructions now have deterministic structural representation.

## Prompt Compression

Observed improvement:

- repeated instructions about localhost-only preview moved into `LOCALHOST_PREVIEW_RUNTIME_V1`;
- repeated instructions about preview lifecycle moved into `GOVERNED_PREVIEW_RUNTIME_EXECUTION_V1`;
- repeated instructions about targeted pytest validation moved into `GOVERNED_TEST_EXECUTION_V1`;
- repeated warnings about deployment, daemon persistence, public binding, shell chaining, and production mutation moved into forbidden boundary checks and finalization evidence.

Replay-safe status:
`IMPROVED`

Governance interpretation:
Prompt text can now reference stable primitives rather than re-declaring every operational constraint.

## Boundedness Preservation

Observed preserved boundaries:

- no deployment automation;
- no daemon persistence;
- no public runtime exposure;
- no unrestricted shell execution;
- no arbitrary subprocess authority;
- no CI/CD authority;
- no production mutation;
- no autonomous operational authority.

Preview helper state:
`server_started: false`

Test helper state:
`executed: false`

Replay-safe status:
`PRESERVED`

Governance interpretation:
Operational acceleration increased without expanding runtime authority.

## Duplicate Prevention

## Operational Entropy Reduction

Entropy reduced by:

- moving repeated operational constraints into deterministic helpers;
- locking preview runtime command preparation;
- locking targeted test command preparation;
- making forbidden boundaries explicit and test-covered;
- improving replay lineage consistency across primitives.

Residual entropy:

- prompts still need short explicit reminders until future Codex instances consistently consult the substrate;
- final reports still repeat explicit limitations for human auditability;
- evidence logs may accumulate unless periodically indexed.

Replay-safe status:
`REDUCED_WITH_RESIDUAL_TEXTUAL_OVERHEAD`

Governance interpretation:
The substrate is reducing operational ambiguity, but evidence organization will matter as more primitives accumulate.

## Current Substrate State

Current state:
`EFFECTIVE_BOUNDED_SUBSTRATE`

Current limitations:

- no command execution by helpers;
- no server lifecycle management;
- no automatic test execution;
- no deployment;
- no orchestration;
- no autonomous primitive generation;
- no self-modifying governance.

## Conclusion

The substrate evolution trend is positive.

The recent cycles demonstrate that SAPIANTA can reduce prompt repetition and operational ambiguity by evolving repeated instructions into bounded, deterministic, replay-visible governance primitives.

The substrate remains constitutionally bounded because it prepares and validates operational intent without granting autonomous execution authority.
